channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend. Then again, nobody could have seen this coming.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia turns green on Valentine’s Day to celebrate Super Bowl champions
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia was awash in green on Valentine’s Day to celebrate its Super Bowl champions.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cam Newton doubles down on saying Panthers had 'locker room of losers' prior to drafting him
item.description: Former Carolina star quarterback Cam Newton doubled down on recent comments that the Panthers had “a locker room of losers” before drafting him with the No. 1 overall pick in 2011, saying his remarks were “aggressive” but “true.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Marcus Stroman reports to Yankees camp and insists he won't go to the bullpen
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Marcus Stroman reported to New York Yankees spring training on Friday after missing the first two days of workouts and said he will refuse to pitch out of the bullpen. “I won’t pitch in the bullpen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: No. 1 UCLA ends its run as nation's lone unbeaten team with upset loss to crosstown rival USC
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The unbeaten streak is over for UCLA. The top-ranked Bruins lost 71-60 to sixth-ranked USC and JuJu Watkins on Thursday night, ending their school-record run of 23 consecutive victories to open the season. They were the nation’s last undefeated women’s team.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bubba Wallace celebrates his 1st qualifier win at Daytona with his newborn
item.description: Wallace, driving the No. 23 Toyota for 23XI Racing, won the first of two qualifying races Thursday for the upcoming Daytona 500. He showed enough speed to make him one of the favorites heading into “The Great American Race.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gold, silver or bronze. Mikaela Shiffrin has never finished off the podium in a slalom at the worlds
item.description: SAALBACH-HINTERGLEMM, Austria (AP) — The good news for Mikaela Shiffrin is that she no longer feels pain in her abdomen 2½ months after a deep puncture wound nearly pierced her colon.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Canadian hockey fans boo Team USA players, national anthem at 4 Nations tournament in Montreal
item.description: Canadian hockey fans booed members of Team USA and the "Star-Spangled Banner" during the team's first game of the 4 Nations Face-Off on Thursday in Montreal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JuJu Watkins lifts sixth-ranked USC to program-defining win over No. 1 UCLA
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins broke out of a scoring slump with 38 points, helping sixth-ranked Southern California topple No. 1 UCLA 71-60 and further delivering on her goal of restoring the program to its earlier glory.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nike unveils Caitlin Clark billboard in city where Angel Reese's team is based, inciting mockery from fans
item.description: Nike unveiled a Caitlin Clark billboard in Chicago this week that it is located just 3.5 miles away from Angel Reese's home WNBA arena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Muni will be free for NBA-All Star, Lunar New Year weekend, SFMTA announces
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- With thousands of people expected to descend on San Francisco this weekend for the NBA All-Stars and Lunar New Year Parade, officials are urging people to leave their cars at home.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Clippers fan hits insane half-court shot to win grand prize
item.description: Ahead of Valentine's Day on Friday, this Los Angeles Clippers fan is reminding you to "shoot your shot.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Southwestern College Baseball Coach Jay Martel Hits 200 Career Wins
item.description: Southwestern College baseball coach Jay Martel swinging for the fences now has hit the 200 win total and counting.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Avery Neff makes stunning recovery from ankle injury
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back January 17th, the #1 gymnastics recruit in the country, freshman Avery Neff was sailing along in her floor routine until her ankle buckled. The Huntsman Center went quiet as Neff had to be carried off the mat. Even she thought her season was over.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party canceled due to weather
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The official NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party, which was set to take place Thursday at Thrive City has been canceled due to weather. The event was due to take place between 5 p.m. and 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Like Father, Like Son; the Neals are a Wrestling family
item.description: The apple doesn't fall far from the tree, and for the Neal family, the apple has landed on the wrestling mats at Poway High School.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC earns first shootout victory over Capitals
item.description: WASHINGTON D.C. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is carrying a ton of momentum into the Four Nations Break. Karel Vejmelka made 30 saves in relief, and the Utah Hockey Club recovered for a 5-4 shootout win against the Washington Capitals at Capital One Arena on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: President Trump arrives at Super Bowl LIX in New Orleans
item.description: In addition to the history that may be made on the field, the U.S. will see a first during Super Bowl LIX: a sitting president will be in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Jets tell Aaron Rodgers they're 'moving on' from the QB for next season, report says
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is still contemplating whether to continue his playing career. If he does, it appears it won’t be with the New York Jets. The team has told the 41-year-old quarterback it is “moving on from him,” Fox Sports reported Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A major Lakers trade was rescinded. Here's why
item.description: The night before the NBA trade deadline, the Los Angeles Lakers parted ways with a young, budding star and a draft pick in exchange for a center.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Clippers dominate fourth quarter in win over Jazz
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - The Los Angeles Clippers broke open a close game in the fourth quarter, outscoring the Jazz 36-24, en route to a 130-110 win over Utah Saturday night. Jordan Clarkson led the Jazz with 24 points, while Keyonte George added 21 off the bench.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Radek Faksa scores in the 10th round of the shootout to give Blues a 6-5 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Radek Faksa scored in the 10th round of the shootout to give the St. Louis Blues a 6-5 win over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: He's back, baby! ESPN's Dick Vitale makes return to commentating following 4th bout with cancer
item.description: ESPN’s Dick Vitale received a standing ovation Saturday night at Clemson’s Littlejohn Coliseum in his first game back as a commentator since recovering from his fourth bout with cancer.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC gives up season-high 7 goals to Carolina
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (ABC4 Sports) - After two overtime thrilling victories, the Utah Hockey Club gave up a season-high seven goals in a 7-3 loss Saturday to the Carolina Hurricanes. Seth Jarvis had two goals and two assists, and Sebastian Aho had two goals and an assist for the Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how to watch the Super Bowl for free
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles will face off in Super Bowl LIX, two years after their matchup in Super Bowl LVII, which the Chiefs won 38-35.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, NewsNation

item.title: White Sox agree to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago White Sox have agreed to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury that includes an invitation to big league spring training camp. The team announced the move on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
